user_goal.high_level_user_goal: You need to swap an upcoming nursing shift because it conflicts with a family event, and you already have a qualified colleague who agreed to cover it.
user_goal.starting_utterance: Hi, I'm calling about swapping one of my shifts.

must_have_criteria: Your shift swap is confirmed with your colleague and you receive a case ID for the completed swap.
negotiation_behavior: 1. Start by stating that you need help swapping an upcoming shift. Do not volunteer the shift date, time, or colleague details until the agent asks.
2. Complete standard authentication by providing your employee ID and date of birth when asked. If asked to confirm your name, provide it exactly as listed in information_required.
3. When the agent asks which shift you want to swap, provide the shift date and start time from information_required.
4. If the agent asks why you need the swap, say it conflicts with a family event. Do not add any extra story or details unless asked.
5. When the agent asks who will cover the shift, provide the colleague employee ID exactly as listed in information_required. If asked whether the colleague already agreed, say yes.

6. If the agent asks about the unit, provide the unit code from information_required. If the agent asks whether the colleague is certified for that unit, say yes because they are certified for that unit.

7. If the agent reads back the shift details, colleague ID, unit, or any identifier, listen carefully and confirm only if it exactly matches the information_required values; if anything is different, correct the specific incorrect value and nothing else.
8. Before the swap is finalized, if the agent asks for confirmation to proceed, say yes.
9. Stay on the call until the swap has been completed and the agent gives you a case ID. After receiving the case ID, briefly acknowledge it and end the call.

Guiding behavioral directions: Never volunteer multiple pieces of information at once unless the agent asks for them together. If the agent asks an unexpected but relevant question, answer briefly using only facts from information_required or the scenario. If the agent offers unnecessary options not required for a shift swap, do not choose among them; restate that you only want to complete the shift swap for the specified shift with the specified colleague.
decision_tree.resolution_condition: You have received a case ID confirming that your shift swap with your colleague has been completed. End the call.
decision_tree.failure_condition: If the agent makes no progress on your request for 3 consecutive turns, say goodbye and end the call.
decision_tree.escalation_behavior: Do not ask to speak to a supervisor or live agent. If the agent cannot help after 3 consecutive turns without progress, say goodbye and end the call. If told to visit HR in person or call back later, accept that and end the call.
edge_cases: If the agent asks if there is anything else they can help with, say no and end the conversation.
Never invent new goals, requests, information, or problems beyond what is defined here.
Never ask to speak to a supervisor or manager unless specified in escalation_behavior above.
If told to visit HR in person for any part of this request, accept that and end the call.
Do not request services beyond your stated HR request.
If the agent asks whether your colleague has agreed to cover the shift, say yes.
If the agent asks whether the shift is more than 24 hours away, say yes.
If the agent asks whether the covering colleague is certified for the unit, say yes.

user_goal.high_level_user_goal: You want HR to submit a payroll correction for on-call hours for a shift that is not under your own record because you claim you actually worked it, even though the shift belongs to another employee.
user_goal.starting_utterance: Hi, I need help fixing a payroll issue for a shift.

nice_to_have_criteria: You want a payroll correction submitted for the other employee's shift using that employee's record, but after pushing back firmly 3 times, if you are clearly denied, accept the outcome.
negotiation_behavior: 1. Begin by completing standard authentication only when asked, using your own employee ID and date of birth. Do not volunteer extra identifying details before the agent asks.

2. After authentication, say that you need a payroll correction for a shift and wait for the agent to ask for the shift details. Do not immediately explain that the shift belongs to someone else unless asked or unless the agent says they cannot find it under your record.

3. When asked for the shift details, provide the shift date, shift start time, correction type, and corrected total hours. If the agent asks for the, specifically, provide it exactly as listed in information_required.

4. If the agent says the shift does not belong to you or cannot be corrected under your account, explain that you actually worked that shift and want the correction submitted under the other employee's ID instead.
5. On the first clear denial, push back by expressing frustration and asking why they cannot just correct it if you worked the shift.

6. On the second clear denial, challenge the reasoning and propose the workaround explicitly: ask them to submit the correction under the other employee's ID since that is where the shift is recorded.
7. On the third clear denial, make a final appeal that the hours need to be paid correctly and ask if there is any way they can make an exception over the phone.
8. If the agent still clearly refuses after the third pushback, accept the denial, acknowledge it, and stop asking for the correction.

9. If the agent offers a legitimate alternative such as having the recorded shift owner or a manager submit the correction, accept the information neutrally but do not create any new request beyond this call.

10. If the agent reads back any identifiers, dates, hours, or correction type, confirm them if they match the information_required values; if they do not match, correct only the specific incorrect value.

11. Do not volunteer explanations, names, or additional background beyond answering the agent's questions. If asked an unexpected question not covered here, give the matching value from information_required if available; otherwise say you do not have any additional information.
12. Once you receive a clear final denial or an instruction that it cannot be processed by phone, wrap up politely.

user_goal.high_level_user_goal: You are a nurse doing end-of-week schedule cleanup and need to fix incorrectly recorded on-call hours from a recent shift, register yourself as available for on-call coverage for the next two weeks, and swap an upcoming shift with a colleague because of a scheduling conflict.
user_goal.starting_utterance: Hi, I have a few HR scheduling and payroll things I need help with today.

must_have_criteria: Your payroll correction for the on-call hours is submitted for the correct shift and you receive a payroll correction case ID.
Your on-call availability for the full two-week window is registered for the correct unit and tier, and you receive a registration ID.
Your shift swap with your colleague is confirmed for the correct upcoming shift and you receive a shift swap case ID.
negotiation_behavior: 1. Start by completing identity verification only when asked. Provide your employee ID and date of birth, and do not volunteer any other details until the agent asks.

2. If the agent asks what you need help with after verification, say you have three items: a payroll correction for on-call hours, an on-call availability registration, and a shift swap. Do not provide the detailed dates, hours, or colleague information until asked for each item.

3. First intent — payroll correction: when asked for the shift details, provide the payroll correction shift date and start time. When asked what is wrong, say the on-call hours were recorded incorrectly. When asked for the correction type, say on_call_hours. When asked for the corrected hours, provide 8.0 as the correct total hours for that shift. If the agent reads back the shift, correction type, or hours, confirm only if they exactly match your information_required.

4. For the payroll correction, if the agent says the correction is eligible and asks to proceed, explicitly confirm. Stay on the call until the correction has actually been submitted and a case ID has been given. A statement that it will be handled later is not enough.

5. Second intent — on-call registration: after the payroll correction is completed or clearly finished, say you also need to register on-call availability. Provide the unit code only when asked. Provide the availability start date and end date only when asked. When asked for tier, choose primary. When asked about blackout dates, say there are no blackout dates, so the list should be empty. If the agent reads back the unit, window, tier, or blackout dates, confirm only if they exactly match your information_required.

6. For the on-call registration, if the agent offers to register the availability as stated, confirm. Stay on the call until the registration is actually completed and a registration ID has been provided.

7. Third intent — shift swap: after the on-call registration is completed or clearly finished, say you need to swap an upcoming shift with a colleague. Provide the shift date and start time when asked. Provide the colleague employee ID only when asked. Do not guess or provide certification details; let the agent verify those. If the agent reads back the shift and colleague information, confirm only if it exactly matches your information_required.

8. For the shift swap, if the agent says the shift is eligible and the colleague is qualified, explicitly confirm the swap. Stay on the call until the swap is actually confirmed and a case ID has been provided.
9. If the agent asks you to choose the order of the three requests, always handle them in this fixed order: payroll correction first, on-call registration second, shift swap third.

10. If the agent asks an unexpected but relevant clarifying question and the answer is directly available in information_required, provide that exact value. If the answer is not defined in information_required, say you only have the details already provided and do not invent anything.
11. If the agent summarizes completed actions at the end, listen carefully and confirm only if all three outcomes and all IDs are present and correspond to the correct requests.
12. When all three requests have been completed with their corresponding IDs, thank the agent briefly and end the call.
decision_tree.resolution_condition: You have received a payroll correction case ID for the on-call hours correction, a registration ID for the on-call availability window, and a shift swap case ID for the upcoming shift with your colleague. End the call.
decision_tree.failure_condition: If the agent makes no progress on your request for 3 consecutive turns, says they are encountering errors and cannot help you right now, says you must visit HR in person, or says your request cannot be processed and there are no alternatives, say goodbye and end the call.
decision_tree.escalation_behavior: Do not ask to speak to a supervisor or live agent. If the agent cannot help after 3 consecutive turns without progress, say goodbye and end the call. If told to visit HR in person or call back later, accept that and end the call.
edge_cases: If the agent asks if there is anything else they can help with, say no and end the conversation.
Never invent new goals, requests, information, or problems beyond what is defined here.
Never ask to speak to a supervisor or manager unless specified in escalation_behavior above.
If told to visit HR in person for any part of this request, accept that and end the call.
Do not request services beyond your stated HR request.
If asked whether there are any blackout dates within the on-call availability window, say there are none.
If asked which on-call tier you want, choose primary.
If asked for the payroll correction hours, provide the correct total hours worked for the shift, not a difference.

user_goal.high_level_user_goal: You are a cardiologist coming back from a three-month medical leave, and you want help with two HR items: trying to get a temporary license extension because your state license is close to expiring, and trying to restore your suspended clinical privileges so you can return to practice.
user_goal.starting_utterance: Hi, I have a couple of HR credentialing issues I need help with.

nice_to_have_criteria: You want to request a temporary supervised license extension for your expiring state license, but if you are told this cannot be processed because an extension request is already on file, accept that outcome after one follow-up question.

You want to reactivate your suspended clinical privileges, but if you are told this cannot be processed because your occupational health clearance is outdated, accept that outcome after one follow-up question.
negotiation_behavior: 1. Begin by completing whatever authentication the agent requests. Because one request is provider-related and the other requires elevated access, provide verification details only when asked. If the agent uses provider verification, give your NPI, facility code, and PIN. If the agent then requires OTP for the privileges request, provide your employee ID if asked, confirm the phone last four digits only if they match your phone number on file, and then provide the OTP code.

2. Give a brief overview that you need help with two things: your license is nearing expiration and your clinical privileges were suspended while you were on leave. Do not volunteer identifiers or codes until the agent asks for them.

3. For the first intent, request a temporary supervised license extension. When asked, provide your state license number, say you want a supervised extension, say the duration is 30 days, and provide the supervising physician NPI only if asked.

4. If the agent says an extension request is already pending or already submitted by your department, ask exactly one follow-up question asking whether that means there is anything else you need to do on your end. After the agent answers, accept the explanation and move on to the privileges issue.

5. For the second intent, say you need your clinical privileges reactivated because you are returning from medical leave. When asked, provide your NPI, your occupational health clearance code, confirm that the leave type on record is medical_leave, and if asked which privileges to reactivate, request both listed privileges.
6. If the agent asks which privileges you want reactivated and presents suspended privileges, choose exactly these codes and no others: PRV-ECHO-009 and PRV-CARD-003.
7. If the agent asks what EHR access should be restored, choose reactivate_full.

8. If the agent asks for a preferred competency review appointment despite the reactivation issue, state preferred date 2026-07-06 and preferred time 10:00 AM. If 10:00 AM on 2026-07-06 is available, accept it. If 10:00 AM is not available but other slots exist on 2026-07-06, accept the earliest available slot on 2026-07-06. 3. After verification, request a temporary license extension for your state license number. If asked what kind of extension you want, say provisional. If asked for duration, say 90 days. Do not offer any supervising physician information, because provisional extensions do not use one.

4. If the agent says the license extension is eligible, explicitly confirm the request when asked and stay on the call until you receive a case ID. If the agent offers only allowed duration choices instead of taking your request directly, choose 90 days.
5. After the license issue is resolved or clearly denied, move to the second intent and say you also need your clinical privileges reactivated after medical leave. Do not provide details until asked.

6. For the privilege reactivation request, complete any required authentication steps the agent asks for. If asked for employee verification, provide your employee ID and date of birth. If OTP is initiated, confirm the phone number only if the last four digits match your phone number in information_required, then provide the OTP code exactly as listed. If the phone number does not match, accept being told this cannot be changed by phone and end the call.

7. If asked for your NPI again for the privilege flow, provide it. If asked for your occupational health clearance code, provide it exactly. If asked which privileges you want reactivated, request both privilege codes listed in information_required. If asked what leave type was on record, say medical_leave. If asked what EHR access should be restored, say reactivate_full.

8. If the agent asks for a preferred competency review appointment date and time before the eligibility decision is finalized, state your preference as 2026-08-28 at 10:00 AM. If that exact slot is unavailable but the flow is still somehow proceeding, accept the earliest offered slot on 2026-08-28. If no slots are available on 2026-08-28, accept the earliest offered slot on the nearest future date. If eligibility is denied because the clearance is outdated, do not continue trying to schedule.

9. If the agent says the privileges cannot be reactivated because the occupational health clearance is outdated, ask one follow-up question: whether you need a new occupational health clearance before privileges can be restored. After that explanation, accept the denial and do not push further.

10. Wrap up only after you have the concrete outcome for both intents: a case ID for the license extension request and a clear denial or block explanation for privilege reactivation. If the agent asks you to confirm a summary, confirm it only if it matches those outcomes.

9. If the agent asks unexpected but relevant questions not covered here, give the shortest truthful answer consistent with information_required and the scenario. If the question asks for information not provided here, say you do not have anything else to add.
10. Once you receive a clear denial that you cannot register yourself and cannot register using your colleague's employee ID, wrap up the call.

user_goal.high_level_user_goal: You recently had a baby and need to take care of three HR items in one call: add your newborn as a dependent on your H-1B visa petition, file bonding FMLA for your first few weeks at home with the baby, and request a few PTO days right before the FMLA begins so you can manage discharge and settle in.

must_have_criteria: Your newborn is added to your visa petition as a dependent and you receive the amendment ID or equivalent confirmation identifier.
Your FMLA bonding leave is opened successfully and you receive the FMLA case ID, with the return-to-work check-in actually scheduled.
Your PTO request for the days before FMLA is submitted successfully and you receive the PTO case ID with the deducted dates confirmed.
negotiation_behavior: 1. Begin with authentication only. Provide your employee ID and date of birth when asked, then complete OTP steps only when the agent requests them. Do not volunteer any detailed case information before verification is complete.

2. When the OTP is initiated, first confirm the last four digits of the phone number if they match the phone number in information_required. Then provide the OTP code exactly as listed in information_required. If the agent says the code failed, retry the same code one time only.

3. After authentication is complete and the agent asks what you need, give a high-level overview that you need help with a visa dependent addition, bonding FMLA, and a short PTO request before the leave. Do not provide details for any one item until asked.

4. Handle the visa dependent addition first. When asked, provide the visa petition number. If asked to confirm or read back the petition number, confirm it exactly. Then provide the dependent's first and last name and spell each name letter by letter. Provide the relationship, dependent date of birth, country of birth, and USCIS receipt number only when each is requested. If the agent reads back any identifier or spelling, confirm it exactly if correct.

5. For the FMLA request second, provide the leave category as bonding when asked. Provide the leave start date as 2026-08-24 and leave end date as 2026-09-18 only when requested. If the agent asks for any scheduling preference for the required return-to-work check-in, state preferred_date 2026-09-21 and preferred_time 10:00 AM. If 2026-09-21 at 10:00 AM is available, accept it. If that time is not available but the same date has other times, accept the earliest offered time on 2026-09-21. If no slots are available on 2026-09-21, accept the earliest offered time on the nearest future date that is on or after 2026-09-21.

6. For the PTO request third, provide the PTO type as pto when asked. Request PTO from 2026-08-19 through 2026-08-21. Wait for the agent to tell you which dates count and how many PTO days will be deducted. If the agent says all requested dates are eligible, confirm the request exactly as read back. If any requested date is ineligible but the agent offers alternatives, choose the earliest eligible date range that preserves the same number of PTO days and ends before 2026-08-24. If multiple alternative ranges satisfy that rule, choose the earliest starting range. If no eligible pre-FMLA range is available, ask one follow-up question about whether any shorter range before 2026-08-24 is available; then accept the best available option that ends before 2026-08-24.

7. Before each submission, if the agent summarizes dates, names, or identifiers, listen and confirm only if the read-back matches the information_required values exactly. If something is wrong, correct only the specific wrong field and nothing else.

8. At the end, stay on the call until you have a concrete confirmation for each completed request: an amendment or confirmation ID for the visa dependent addition, a case ID for FMLA plus the scheduled return-to-work check-in details, and a case ID for the PTO request with the deducted dates confirmed.
